nodes.name: minimize_bandwidth
nodes.description: Ingested pipeline from minimize_bandwidth

nodes.name: BandwidthMetricEvaluation
nodes.description: Compute the bandwidth of a matrix ordering as a pure diagnostic metric.

inputs.name: mat
inputs.constraints: Square or rectangular matrix-like input with indexable rows/columns.

outputs.name: bandwidth
outputs.constraints: Non-negative bandwidth value.

nodes.conceptual_summary: Index-Offset Span Diagnostic. The operation inspects a 2D numeric tensor as an indexed interaction map and locates all structurally active coordinates. It then computes the maximum absolute row-column index offset across those coordinates, which is the smallest diagonal-centered span that contains the active pattern under the current index ordering. The output is a single diagnostic scalar and does not modify, compress, or reorder the input.. Applications: Evaluating node-order quality in discretized partial-differential-equation systems before using banded linear solvers, Assessing linearization locality of interaction graphs in biomolecular or ecological network simulations, Measuring dependency spread in reordered Jacobian/Hessian structures for large-scale optimization pipelines, Estimating memory-locality friendliness of sparse operator layouts in hardware-accelerated scientific computing

nodes.name: ValidateSquareMatrixShape
nodes.description: Check that the input matrix has identical row and column counts; fail if not square.

outputs.name: square_mat
outputs.constraints: same data as input, guaranteed square

nodes.name: ComputeAbsoluteWeightedIndexDistances
nodes.description: Enumerate all entries and compute absolute weighted index distance for each element as abs(value * (row_index - col_index)).

outputs.name: weighted_distances
outputs.constraints: non-negative, one value per matrix entry

nodes.type_signature: (square_mat: 2D numeric ndarray) -> sequence of numeric values

nodes.name: AggregateMaximumDistanceAsBandwidth
nodes.description: Take the maximum weighted distance and convert it to float as the final bandwidth metric.

inputs.name: weighted_distances
inputs.constraints: non-empty if matrix has at least one element

nodes.name: CoreBandwidthReduction
nodes.description: Primary entry-point routine to reduce matrix bandwidth via deterministic reordering.

outputs.name: reordered_matrix
outputs.constraints: Same shape as input with reordered rows/columns.

nodes.conceptual_summary: Deterministic Bi-Axis Locality-Preserving Permutation. Computes a deterministic index permutation for a 2D numeric array and applies that permutation to both axes to relabel element ordering. The transform minimizes interaction distance from the main diagonal, concentrating nonzero or high-magnitude couplings into a narrower band. Numeric entries are not modified; only their coordinates are reassigned through a structure-preserving permutation.. Applications: Sparse linear-system preprocessing in computational mechanics to improve factorization efficiency, Connectivity-matrix reordering in electronic circuit simulation to reduce memory traffic in solvers, Node relabeling in large biological interaction graphs to expose near-diagonal block structure for faster iterative computation

nodes.name: ValidateSymmetricInput
nodes.description: Verify the matrix is numerically symmetric within tolerance before any reordering logic proceeds.

outputs.name: symmetric_matrix
outputs.constraints: Same values as input; guaranteed symmetric or function fails

nodes.name: InitializeReductionState
nodes.description: Create the sanitized working matrix and initial optimization state for greedy bandwidth descent.

outputs.name: iteration_state
outputs.constraints: working_matrix=abs(copy), accumulated_permutation=identity permutation, bandwidth=matrix_bandwidth(working_matrix), remaining_iterations=100

nodes.type_signature: (symmetric_matrix: np.ndarray) -> object {working_matrix: np.ndarray, accumulated_permutation: list[int], bandwidth: int, remaining_iterations: int}

nodes.name: ProposeGreedyPermutationStep
nodes.description: From the current working matrix, compute one candidate permutation and its resulting matrix bandwidth.

inputs.name: iteration_state
inputs.constraints: Contains current working_matrix and associated state

outputs.name: current_iteration_state
outputs.constraints: Pass-through of input state for synchronized evaluation

outputs.name: candidate_permutation
outputs.constraints: Produced by minimize_bandwidth_global on current working_matrix

outputs.name: candidate_matrix
outputs.constraints: permute_matrix(current working_matrix, candidate_permutation)

outputs.name: candidate_bandwidth
outputs.constraints: matrix_bandwidth(candidate_matrix)

nodes.name: UpdateStateWithImprovementCriterion
nodes.description: Apply iteration budget checks, compare candidate vs current bandwidth, and either accept the candidate state or terminate without update.

inputs.name: candidate_matrix
inputs.constraints: Permuted working matrix for this iteration

outputs.name: next_iteration_state
outputs.constraints: Counter decremented; if candidate improves bandwidth, state is updated, otherwise state remains effectively terminal

outputs.name: continue_search
outputs.constraints: True only when candidate_bandwidth < current bandwidth and budget not exhausted

nodes.type_signature: (current_iteration_state: object, candidate_permutation: list[int], candidate_matrix: np.ndarray, candidate_bandwidth: int) -> tuple[object, bool]

nodes.name: ExtractFinalPermutation
nodes.description: Return the accumulated permutation from the terminal state as the function result.

outputs.name: acc_permutation
outputs.constraints: Final accumulated row/column reordering permutation

nodes.name: ThresholdConstrainedBandwidthReduction
nodes.description: Reduce bandwidth only when or until a threshold-related condition is satisfied.

inputs.name: threshold
inputs.constraints: Bandwidth cutoff/target used to gate optimization.

outputs.name: reordered_matrix
outputs.constraints: Output may be unchanged if threshold condition is not met.

nodes.type_signature: (mat: 2D numeric matrix, threshold: numeric scalar) -> 2D numeric matrix
nodes.conceptual_summary: Threshold-Gated Index-Permutation Bandwidth Minimization. Applies an index reordering to a 2D numeric array to reduce its bandwidth metric (the maximum distance of significant entries from a reference diagonal) only when a scalar gate condition is active. The procedure performs constrained optimization with early stopping once the target threshold condition is met, or skips modification when activation criteria are not satisfied. The transform is lossless with respect to numeric values: it changes index order, not entry magnitudes.. Applications: Sparse linear-system preprocessing in computational physics to improve locality and reduce downstream factorization overhead only when sparsity spread exceeds a preset bound., Permutation of large interaction matrices in neuroscience simulations to cluster nonzero couplings near the diagonal for bounded-latency time stepping., Reindexing transition operators in formal verification/model checking so nonzero transitions remain near-diagonal when a complexity threshold is exceeded., Arrangement of pairwise correlation matrices in remote-sensing or imaging pipelines to keep significant coefficients near local neighborhoods under memory-bandwidth constraints.

nodes.name: EnforceThresholdSparsity
nodes.description: Create a thresholded copy of the input matrix where all entries below the threshold are set to zero, preserving the original input matrix.

inputs.name: mat
inputs.constraints: Square, symmetric expected by downstream ordering step

inputs.name: threshold
inputs.constraints: Values strictly below this are zeroed

outputs.name: thresholded_matrix
outputs.constraints: Same shape as mat; entries < threshold replaced with 0

nodes.type_signature: (mat: 2D numeric matrix, threshold: numeric scalar) -> 2D numeric matrix

nodes.name: BuildSparseGraphView
nodes.description: Convert the thresholded dense matrix into a CSR sparse representation suitable for graph-based bandwidth reduction.

inputs.name: thresholded_matrix
inputs.constraints: Numeric matrix; typically sparse after thresholding

outputs.name: sparse_matrix
outputs.constraints: Equivalent numeric structure to thresholded_matrix

nodes.name: ComputeSymmetricBandwidthReducingOrder
nodes.description: Run reverse Cuthill-McKee in symmetric mode on the sparse matrix and materialize the permutation as a NumPy array.

outputs.name: reordered_matrix
outputs.constraints: Permutation of row/column indices for reduced bandwidth

nodes.type_signature: (sparse_matrix: CSR sparse matrix) -> 1D integer permutation array

nodes.name: GlobalBandwidthOptimization
nodes.description: Perform a global optimization pass to minimize matrix bandwidth across the full structure.

inputs.name: mat
inputs.constraints: Matrix-like input suitable for global reordering.

outputs.name: reordered_matrix
outputs.constraints: Globally optimized ordering result.

nodes.conceptual_summary: Global Sparse Structure Linearization. Interprets a 2D numeric array as a weighted relation graph and computes a global index permutation that concentrates significant entries near the main diagonal. The same permutation is applied consistently to structural axes, preserving algebraic equivalence while reducing matrix bandwidth and related profile metrics. The output is topologically identical in connectivity but re-embedded for improved locality in subsequent computations.. Applications: Sparse linear-system preconditioning in computational mechanics (e.g., stiffness/operator matrices)., Node reindexing for large-scale circuit or power-network simulation matrices to improve sparse factorization locality., Permutation of dependency matrices in high-performance task scheduling to cluster near-neighbor interactions., Reordering contact/interaction matrices in biological network analysis to expose near-diagonal block structure.

nodes.name: validate_symmetric_input
nodes.description: Verify the input matrix is symmetric within tolerance and fail fast if the precondition is violated.

outputs.name: validated_mat
outputs.constraints: symmetric within atol=1e-8

nodes.name: build_threshold_search_space
nodes.description: Compute matrix amplitude from absolute values and construct the truncation sweep used to explore candidate permutations.

outputs.name: mat_amplitude
outputs.constraints: ptp(abs(validated_mat).ravel())

outputs.name: truncation_values
outputs.constraints: range [0.1, 1.0) with step 0.01

nodes.name: enumerate_threshold_based_permutations
nodes.description: Generate candidate permutations by calling thresholded bandwidth minimization for each scaled truncation value.

outputs.name: candidate_permutations
outputs.constraints: each item is a valid row/column permutation

nodes.type_signature: (validated_mat: np.ndarray (2D numeric), mat_amplitude: float, truncation_values: np.ndarray (1D float)) -> iterable[np.ndarray | list[int]]

nodes.name: select_minimum_bandwidth_permutation
nodes.description: Evaluate each candidate by permuting the matrix and computing resulting bandwidth, then return the best permutation as a Python list.

outputs.name: optimal_permutation
outputs.constraints: argmin over matrix_bandwidth(permute_matrix(validated_mat, perm))

nodes.type_signature: (validated_mat: np.ndarray (2D numeric), candidate_permutations: iterable[np.ndarray | list[int]]) -> list[int]
